title: Fix timeframe display bug and results screen layout matching reference design

requirements.text: Fix the timeframe display bug in the Results screen: the 'Time Frame' tile in the 2×2 info grid must display the timeframe actually selected by the user in Settings (M1, M3, or M5), not always default to M1. The timeframe must be read from the analysis result's 'timeframe' field (which is populated by localCandleAnalysis.ts reading from localStorage/settings). Additionally, update the countdown timer initialization so M1=60s, M3=180s, M5=300s — matching the selected timeframe exactly. The 'Entrar às HH:MM' static label must compute entry time as current time at page load plus the correct duration for the selected timeframe.

quotes: parece que o app ainda não é restrito mesmo selecionando opção de operar 5minutos de tempo, ele lê 1m, e conta regressiva de 1m
ao ser selecionado, salvar e se o cliente selecionar 5 ou 3 minutos, ser o que está selecionado e ter contagem regressiva

acceptanceCriteria: When user selects M5 in Settings, Results screen shows 'M5' in the Time Frame tile
When user selects M3 in Settings, Results screen shows 'M3' in the Time Frame tile
When user selects M1 in Settings, Results screen shows 'M1' in the Time Frame tile
Countdown timer starts at 300 seconds (05:00) when M5 is selected
Countdown timer starts at 180 seconds (03:00) when M3 is selected
Countdown timer starts at 60 seconds (01:00) when M1 is selected
'Entrar às HH:MM' label shows correct entry time based on selected timeframe duration

requirements.text: Fix the timeframe propagation in frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts: when reading the user's selected timeframe from localStorage/settings, ensure the value is read at analysis execution time (not at module load time). The key used to read from localStorage must exactly match the key used by the Settings page to persist the timeframe. If the stored value is 'M3', 'M5', or 'M1', store it verbatim in the LocalAnalysisResult's 'timeframe' field. Never fall back to 'M1' unless no stored value exists.

quotes: ao ser selecionado, salvar e se o cliente selecionar 5 ou 3 minutos, ser o que está selecionado

acceptanceCriteria: localCandleAnalysis.ts reads timeframe from localStorage at call time, not at import time
The localStorage key used in localCandleAnalysis.ts matches exactly the key used in Settings.tsx to persist the timeframe
result.timeframe is 'M5' when user had M5 selected in Settings
result.timeframe is 'M3' when user had M3 selected in Settings
result.timeframe is 'M1' when user had M1 selected in Settings
result.timeframe defaults to 'M1' only if no value is found in localStorage

requirements.text: Update the Results screen layout to match the reference design shown in image-11.png and image-12.png. The layout must show: (1) Two side-by-side tiles at the top — left tile shows 'TEMPO' label with countdown timer in large bold white (e.g., '00:29') and 'restante' subtitle; right tile shows 'ENTRAR ÀS' label with the entry time in cyan/teal (e.g., '19:10') and 'horário de entrada' subtitle; (2) A large full-width green banner for COMPRA (with '↗ COMPRA' text) or red for VENDA, showing 'Tendência: ALTA/BAIXA', 'Confiança: XX%', and a 'Força: forte/média/fraca' badge inline within the banner; (3) A 2×2 grid of dark tiles showing Tendência (with colored arrow), Precisão (with value), Volume (with value), Time Frame (with selected timeframe value); (4) The chart thumbnail displayed below the grid as a scrollable secondary section with support/resistance overlays.

quotes: deixar tambem entrada com o que selecionado, time frame

acceptanceCriteria: Top section shows two dark tiles side by side: TEMPO countdown and ENTRAR ÀS time
TEMPO tile displays MM:SS countdown in large bold white text with 'restante' label below
ENTRAR ÀS tile displays entry time in cyan/teal with 'horário de entrada' label below
Signal banner is full-width green for COMPRA, red for VENDA
Signal banner displays Tendência, Confiança percentage, and Força badge inline
2×2 info grid shows Tendência, Precisão, Volume, and Time Frame tiles
Time Frame tile reflects the user-selected timeframe (M1/M3/M5)
Layout matches the reference screenshots (image-11.png, image-12.png)

requirements.text: Fix the Settings page timeframe selector in frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx to correctly persist the selected value (M1, M3, or M5) to localStorage and to the backend via the updateSettings mutation. Audit the localStorage key name used by Settings.tsx when saving the timeframe and ensure it is identical to the key read by localCandleAnalysis.ts. After saving, show the Portuguese success toast 'Configuração salva!'. Verify that selecting M3 or M5 and navigating away then returning to Settings still shows the previously saved value (not reverted to M1).

quotes: selecionando opção de operar 5minutos de tempo, ele lê 1m
ao ser selecionado, salvar e se o cliente selecionar 5 ou 3 minutos, ser o que está selecionado

acceptanceCriteria: Selecting M5 in Settings and saving stores 'M5' in localStorage under the correct key
Selecting M3 in Settings and saving stores 'M3' in localStorage under the correct key
Navigating away and returning to Settings shows the previously selected timeframe
Toast 'Configuração salva!' appears after each successful save
The localStorage key in Settings.tsx exactly matches the key read in localCandleAnalysis.ts

requirements.text: Ensure the analysis explanation text (explicacao field) generated by frontend/src/services/localCandleAnalysis.ts is unique per analysis and meaningfully references the specific detected patterns, trend direction, and candle characteristics found in the particular image being analyzed. Prevent generic or repetitive phrases from being reused across different chart analyses by incorporating the actual computed values (trend direction, pattern names, confidence score, momentum count) into the explanation template dynamically.

quotes: sobre Análise Técnica escrever no que o print do gráfico quer dizer realmente, pois detectei frases iguais em gráficos diferentes amigo

acceptanceCriteria: Two different chart images analyzed back-to-back produce different explicacao texts
The explicacao text references the specific detected pattern names (e.g., 'Engolfo de Alta', 'Martelo')
The explicacao text references the detected trend direction (ALTA/BAIXA/LATERAL)
The explicacao text references the computed confidence or momentum value
The explicacao text uses professional Portuguese trading analyst language
No hardcoded static string is returned regardless of input — the text varies with analysis results

constraints: Do not change the backend schema or Motoko actor beyond what is strictly necessary for timeframe persistence
All user-facing text must remain in Portuguese (Brazil)
The localStorage key for timeframe storage must be unified and consistent across Settings.tsx and localCandleAnalysis.ts
Do not reintroduce any external API (Gemini, OpenAI) calls — analysis must remain fully local
nonGoals: Adding new analysis features beyond what is already implemented
Changing the local candle pixel-analysis algorithm logic
Modifying the processing screen animation speed or design
Adding new settings options beyond the existing M1/M3/M5 timeframe selector
